translation_match: Which labeled polygon is the translation of the Reference by the shown vector?
Identify the labeled polygon that matches the Reference after translating it by the shown vector.
Use the shown vector. Which labeled polygon is the translated image of the Reference?
Which labeled polygon is the image of the Reference after the shown translation?
Apply the shown vector to the Reference. Which labeled polygon matches the result?
reflection_match: Which labeled polygon is the reflection of the Reference across line L?
Reflect the Reference across line L. Which labeled polygon matches the result?
Use line L as the reflection line. Which labeled polygon is the reflected image of the Reference?
Which labeled polygon matches the Reference after reflecting it across line L?
Identify the labeled polygon produced by reflecting the Reference across line L.
rotation_match: Which labeled polygon is the image of the Reference after a {rotation_instruction} about point O?
Rotate the Reference by a {rotation_instruction} about point O. Which labeled polygon matches the result?
Using point O as the center, which labeled polygon matches the Reference after a {rotation_instruction}?
Which labeled polygon is produced by a {rotation_instruction} of the Reference about point O?
Apply a {rotation_instruction} about point O to the Reference. Which labeled polygon is correct?

code_prompt_defaults.object_description: a Reference polygon, one transformation cue, and labeled candidate polygons on a grid
code_prompt_defaults.json_output_contract: Return a JSON object with keys "answer" and "annotation".
code_prompt_defaults.json_output_contract_answer_only: Return a JSON object with key "answer".
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_template: set "annotation" to an array of exactly {vertex_count} pixel points [x,y] for the winning polygon vertices
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint: set "answer" to the single-letter label of the correct polygon
